one_liner: TSM at $437 reports earnings July 16 with guidance for $39-40B Q2 revenue and 65.5-67.5% gross margin — one of the strongest earnings setups on the watchlist with Grade A fundamentals and AI chip demand described as 'extremely robust.'

price_action.intraday_story: TSM opened at $446.73, immediately hit a high of $447.69 then sold off steadily throughout the session to close at $436.96 near the low of $436.39. This is a bearish intraday pattern — gap-up then fade — but in the context of pre-earnings positioning and the broader semiconductor sector noise, the pullback may represent normal profit-taking ahead of the July 16 catalyst.

news_catalyst.headline: TSM Q2 Earnings July 16: Guided $39-40B Revenue, 65.5-67.5% Gross Margin; AI Demand 'Extremely Robust'
news_catalyst.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor reports Q2 earnings on July 16 — the same day as Netflix. Management guided Q2 revenues of $39-40.2B (10% sequential growth) with gross margins of 65.5-67.5%. The company described agentic AI demand as 'extremely robust' and is ramping 2-nanometer production. TSM stock surged 39.9% in the April-June quarter. The July 16 report will be the first major semiconductor earnings of the season and will set the tone for the sector. The ADR DCF model is distorted (showing 4519% upside due to share count differences between ADR and underlying shares) — this should be disregarded.

technical.macd_signal: Slightly bearish — MACD histogram -2.072, negative but narrowing
technical.summary:
TSM is in an uptrend above both EMAs (EMA50 $426.03, EMA200 $413.65) but today's session showed intraday weakness — opened near the high and closed near the low. RSI at 51.2 is neutral. MACD histogram at -2.072 is slightly negative, suggesting the recent run has decelerated.

The Wyckoff trading range support at $364.29 and resistance at $465.38 frames a stock in the upper half of its range but not yet at the ceiling. The EMA50 at $426.03 is the first key support on any further pullback.

The July 16 earnings report is the dominant factor overriding all technical signals. The management's pre-guidance of $39-40B revenue and 65-67% gross margins sets a high bar — any beat on these figures would likely send the stock toward $465 resistance. A miss or soft guidance on 2027 AI demand would break the $426 EMA50 support.

wyckoff_narrative:
TSM's neutral Wyckoff classification reflects the post-rally consolidation. After a 39.9% Q2 run, the stock is digesting gains above its EMAs without clear accumulation or distribution signals. The current pattern — holding above EMA50 with declining MACD but stable RSI — is consistent with Phase B consolidation before the next move.

The earnings event on July 16 will be the decisive Wyckoff event: either a Sign of Strength (SOS) thrust on positive results, or a Sign of Weakness (SOW) break if AI demand commentary disappoints. Given management's pre-guided numbers are already strong, the market will be focused on 2027 AI demand outlook and 2nm production ramp commentary.

valuation.summary: DCF value of $20,184 is a model artifact — TSM ADRs represent a fractional share count of the underlying Taiwan-listed shares, creating a per-share distortion. This DCF figure should be disregarded. TSM is fundamentally valued by the market at a premium to historical multiples given its AI chip monopoly on leading-edge manufacturing.

key_strengths: Highest fundamental grade on watchlist at 0.89 — near-monopoly on advanced node manufacturing
Guided 65.5-67.5% gross margins for Q2 — exceptional for a capital-intensive manufacturer
AI chip demand described by management as 'extremely robust' — backs up the narrative
key_concerns: Geopolitical risk: Taiwan Strait tensions could cause significant premium compression
Concentration risk: high dependency on Apple and Nvidia as top customers
fundamentals.summary: TSM has the highest fundamental grade (A, 0.89) on the watchlist. Its monopoly on leading-edge chip manufacturing for Apple, Nvidia, AMD, and others creates a durable competitive moat. Fundamentals strongly support the watch-into-accumulate stance pending the July 16 earnings confirmation.

risks: Earnings July 16 — miss on revenue or margin guidance, or soft 2027 AI demand commentary, would break $426 EMA50
Geopolitical risk: Taiwan Strait tensions are a permanent tail risk for this stock
MACD histogram negative today (-2.072) — momentum slightly bearish heading into earnings
